chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلَيْنِ يَدَّعِيَانِ فَيُقِيمُ كُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا الْبَيِّنَةَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 16 – Two men both claiming, so each one of the two establishes the proof

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/288)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/288)
text: شَاهِدَيْنِ شَهِدَا عَلَى أَمْرٍ وَاحِدٍ وَ جَاءَ آخَرَانِ فَشَهِدَا عَلَى غَيْرِ الَّذِي شَهِدَا وَ اخْتَلَفُوا قَالَ يُقْرَعُ بَيْنَهُمْ فَأَيُّهُمْ قُرِعَ عَلَيْهِ الْيَمِينُ فَهُوَ أَوْلَى بِالْقَضَاءِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Moala Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa, from Dawood Bin Sirham,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding two witnesses who testified upon one matter and two others came over, so they testified upon other than which they had testified, and they differed. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Lots would be drawn between them, so whoever the lot comes out to for the swearing of the oath, so he would be the first to be judged for’.
en.sarwar: 4. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad ibn Muhammad from al-Washsha’ from Dawud ibn Sarhan who has narrated the following: “About the case of two witnesses who testify about one thing and two others testify differently, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that a raffle must be arranged and whoever ’s name comes out to take an oath then judgment must be issued in his favor.’”
